creator: nsayer@quack.kfu.com
text:
Solaris defaults to using NFS V3. Aparently, so does this snapshot, since
I did nothing I know of in the kernel config to ask for it (options NFS,
FAILSAFE among others, but nothing about V3)

How-To-Repeat: 
solaris# mount -o rw,soft,bg,intr freebsd:/foo /mnt
solaris# cd /mnt
solaris# ls

At this point the FreeBSD machine panics with

ufs_ihashget: recursive lock not expected -- pid %d

Adding vers=2 to the -o list in the mount prevents this.
